task_completion.description: Configure the trigger for the Automated Storage Health Alert.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Nx Witness Event Rules dialog, there should be a rule where the 'Event' is set to 'Storage Failure' (or 'Storage Issue') and the 'Source' or 'Server' field is set to 'Any Server'.

task_completion.description: Configure the HTTP POST action and URL for the Storage Health Alert.
task_completion.visual_evidence: For the Storage Failure rule, the 'Action' must be set to 'Do HTTP request'. The 'HTTP Method' must be 'POST' and the 'URL' field must exactly match 'https://ops-api.internal/webhooks/health'.

task_completion.description: Configure the JSON payload body for the Storage Health Alert.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'HTTP Content' or 'Body' field for the Storage Failure rule contains the required JSON: {"type": "CRITICAL_FAILURE", "component": "Storage", ...}. Note: Accept either {event.sourceName} or {event.source} for the server field.

task_completion.description: Configure the Soft Trigger for the Operator Panic Button.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Event Rules dialog, there should be a rule where 'Event' is 'Soft Trigger'. The 'Name' field must be 'Panic Alert', the icon selected should be a Bell, and it should be configured to be available to all users and cameras.

task_completion.description: Configure the HTTP POST action and URL for the Panic Alert.
task_completion.visual_evidence: For the Panic Alert rule, the 'Action' must be set to 'Do HTTP request'. The 'HTTP Method' must be 'POST' and the 'URL' field must exactly match 'https://ops-api.internal/webhooks/panic'.

task_completion.description: Configure the JSON payload body for the Panic Alert.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'HTTP Content' or 'Body' field for the Panic Alert rule contains the required JSON: {"priority": "HIGH", "alert_type": "PANIC_BUTTON", ...}. Note: Soft triggers do not support macros, so accept the payload even if {user.name} or {event.cameraId} are omitted, left literal, or modified.

integrity.description: The agent used the Nx Witness VMS graphical user interface to configure the rules.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the actual Nx Witness desktop client and its 'Event Rules' configuration dialog, not a terminal or script.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the expected JSON payloads into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The JSON payloads and URLs must be entered into the specific input fields within the Nx Witness Event Rules dialog, not in Notepad, a terminal, or a web browser.

integrity.description: The configuration appears to be genuinely applied in the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Apply' or 'OK' buttons in the Event Rules dialog should be visible and interacted with, showing the rules are actually being saved to the system.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Health URL is verified as 'https://ops-api.internal/webhooks/health'. Target Panic URL is verified as 'https://ops-api.internal/webhooks/panic'. The Soft Trigger name is 'Panic Alert'. Note: The task description asks for macros like {event.sourceName}, {user.name}, and {event.cameraId}. However, Nx Witness uses {event.source} instead of {event.sourceName}, and Soft Triggers do not support variable macros at all. Do not penalize the agent if it uses {event.source} for the health alert, or if it omits/modifies the unsupported macros in the Soft Trigger body, as long as the base JSON structure is present.
